content.problemStatement:
The young but promising paparazzi Gennady wants to finally become famous. To do this, he wants to take a picture of a new super star during her walk in the mountains.

It is known that the star's path in the mountains consists of NNN sections. For each valid iii, the iii-th section is a vertical half-open interval with coordinates x=ix = ix=i and y‚àà[0,hi)y \in [0, h_i)y‚àà[0,hi‚Äã).

For each valid iii and jjj, our hero can take a picture of the star located in the iii-th section of the walk when he is in the jjj-th section only if he can see the star ‚Äï that is, i<ji \lt ji<j and for each kkk (i<k<ji \lt k \lt ji<k<j), the half-open interval that makes up the kkk-th section must not intersect the line segment [(i,hi),(j,hj)][(i, h_i), (j, h_j)][(i,hi‚Äã),(j,hj‚Äã)].

Gennady is a paparazzi, not a programmer, so he asks you to determine the maximum distance from which he can take a picture of the star, which is the maximum value of j‚àíij-ij‚àíi over all pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j). Help him become famous!
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
**Subtask #2 (10 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 8,0008,0008,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers h1,h2,‚Ä¶,hNh_1, h_2, \ldots, h_Nh1‚Äã,h2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,hN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the maximum distance.
